title: Parameter names should not duplicate the names of their methods
description: Parameter names should not duplicate the names of their methodsAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellconventionconfusingWhy is this an issue?The name of a method should communicate what it does, and the names of its parameters should indicate how they’re used. If a method and its parameter have the same name it is an indication that one of these rules of thumb has been broken, if not both. Even if by some trick of language that’s not the case, it is still likely to confuse callers and maintainers.Noncompliant code examplepublic void Login(string login) // Noncompliant { //..
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects methods where parameter names duplicate the method name
logic.triggering_conditions: MethodDeclaration nodes where a parameter name matches the method name
logic.exclusion_conditions: Excludes unit tests, logging statements, generated code, and constructors
never_trigger_on: Constructors (parameter names often match class names)
logic.reasoning: Detects common cases where parameter names duplicate the method name, which can cause confusion and poor readability. Uses specific patterns for common method names to avoid complex regex. This is a simplified implementation that covers the most common violation patterns.
